title: multidevice: client derives device_id from KeyPackage
description:
Client-side change: before calling publish_key_package, derive device_id from the ECDH encryption key in the freshly generated KeyPackage and include it in the request.

Why: device_id must be stable for the lifetime of a KeyPackage and unique per device. Deriving it from the KeyPackage's own leaf encryption key requires no extra storage and is deterministic — the relay and client agree on it without a round-trip.

What to do:
- In core/src/mls.rs or the CLI publish path, after generating a KeyPackage, extract the leaf node encryption key bytes and compute SHA-256 to produce device_id
- Pass device_id alongside the KeyPackage bytes when constructing PublishKeyPackageParams
- Ensure the same device_id is reproduced if the same KeyPackage is republished in the same session

Acceptance: client publishes a device_id that matches SHA-256 of the KeyPackage leaf encryption key; relay stores it correctly.
close_reason: MlsClient.key_package_and_device_id() added to core/src/mls.rs; derives device_id=SHA-256(hpke_init_key); CLI and TUI call sites updated; sha2 removed from tui Cargo.toml
